text: remaining displays follow one-call pattern require optional packages, shown code: degrades gracefully: optional package missing get clear, actionable message (animate_world() falls back faceted small-multiple).
code: # Bivariate choropleth (two variables at once) — needs `biscale` + `sf` world_data(2020, c(gdp = "NY.GDP.PCAP.KD", life = "SP.DYN.LE00.IN"),            geometry = "sf") |>   bivariate_map(gdp, life)  # Area-honest cartogram — needs `cartogram` + `sf` world_data(2020, c(pop = "SP.POP.TOTL"), geometry = "sf") |>   cartogram_map(pop, type = "dorling")  # Animated choropleth over a year panel — needs `gganimate` world_data(2000:2020, c(gdp = "NY.GDP.PCAP.KD")) |>   animate_world(gdp)  # Interactive choropleth — needs `leaflet`, `ggiraph` or `plotly` world_data(2020) |>   interactive_map(gdp_per_capita, engine = "plotly")

title: countryatlas and ggsql
text: world_query() pure string builder — needs nothing installed — can see exactly sent ggsql:
code: world_query(   gdp_per_capita,   projection = "equal_earth",   palette    = "magma",   transform  = "log10",   title      = "GDP per capita" ) #> VISUALISE gdp_per_capita AS fill #> FROM countryatlas_world #> DRAW spatial #> PROJECT TO equal_earth #> SCALE fill TO magma VIA log10 #> LABEL title => 'GDP per capita'

title: countryatlas and ggsql
text: as_ggsql_source() exports curated countryatlas table (sf geometry WKB-encoded) DuckDB connection, Parquet file, nanoarrow stream ggsql can read. one-call path interactive_map(engine = "ggsql"): hood just two building blocks, can also drive full control query:
code: # needs: ggsql, duckdb, DBI, sf world_data(2020, geometry = "sf") |>   interactive_map(gdp_per_capita, engine = "ggsql", transform = "log10") src <- world_data(2020, geometry = "sf") |>   as_ggsql_source(format = "duckdb")          # a DuckDB connection  q <- world_query(gdp_per_capita, projection = "orthographic", palette = "viridis")  ggsql::ggsql_execute(src, q)                  # -> Vega-Lite widget

title: countryatlas: Joining World Data to Maps on the ISO Spine
text: Joining country-level data across independent sources deceptively hard: country spelled "US", "U.S.", "United States" "United States America", naïve join treats different entities. countryatlas resolves friction adopting ISO 3166 codes universal join key stitching together three otherwise disjoint resources — map geometry, World Bank development indicators, comprehensive country-code crosswalk — single, map-ready table. vignette presents package’s design philosophy, complete functional vocabulary, worked examples spanning data assembly, join engine, diagnostics, reference data, analysis helpers full grammar honest cartographic displays. examples run offline bundled data snapshot.

title: countryatlas: Joining World Data to Maps on the ISO Spine
text: package rests single conviction: task make easier get country data onto map — make map honest — belong . Concretely, three packages combined: ggplot2::map_data("world") (Natural Earth via sf) supplies polygon geometry, .e. countries ; WDI supplies World Bank indicators, .e. true ; countrycode supplies crosswalk ISO codes, continents regions makes reliable join possible. Three design commitments follow. First, happy path one call: world_data(2020) returns tibble ready map. Second, ISO code spine: every function speaks iso3c/iso2c internally exposes , anything package produces joins anything else — user’s data. Third, country lost silently: entities map backends spell idiosyncratically matched curated override table rather dropped, unmatched values reported explicitly. keep every example reproducible without network connection, vignette uses bundled world_snapshot dataset, curated set indicators one recent year.
code: snapshot <- world_snapshot$countries dplyr::glimpse(snapshot) #> Rows: 215 #> Columns: 10 #> $ iso3c           <chr> "AFG", "ALB", "DZA", "ASM", "AND", "AGO", "ATG", "ARG"… #> $ iso2c           <chr> "AF", "AL", "DZ", "AS", "AD", "AO", "AG", "AR", "AM", … #> $ country         <chr> "Afghanistan", "Albania", "Algeria", "American Samoa",… #> $ continent       <chr> "Asia", "Europe", "Africa", "Oceania", "Europe", "Afri… #> $ region          <chr> "South Asia", "Europe & Central Asia", "Middle East & … #> $ income          <fct> Low income, Upper middle income, Upper middle income, … #> $ gdp_per_capita  <dbl> 377.6656, 5867.6510, 4544.4669, 13709.0975, 39780.4153… #> $ population      <dbl> 40578842, 2451636, 45477389, 48342, 79705, 35635029, 9… #> $ life_expectancy <dbl> 65.61700, 78.76900, 76.12900, 72.75200, 84.01600, 64.2… #> $ co2_per_capita  <dbl> 0.278420956, 1.845257616, 4.160058529, 0.002068595, NA…

title: countryatlas: Joining World Data to Maps on the ISO Spine
text: headline function generalised backward-compatible. classic call returns polygon-backed, enriched tibble exactly : indicator argument accepts one many WDI codes; named vector drives clean column names. range years (2000:2020) yields panel keyed iso3c year. geometry argument switches classic "polygon" backend, modern "sf" backend real projections, "none" pure analysis.

title: countryatlas: Joining World Data to Maps on the ISO Spine
text: package’s mission, exposed reader’s data. Given frame keyed messy names, standardize_country() attaches ISO codes classifications: join_world() goes one step — auto-detecting country column, standardising attaching geometry — country_join() reconciles two independent tables key country names:
code: messy <- data.frame(   nation = c("U.S.", "S. Korea", "Czechia", "Kosovo", "Cote d'Ivoire"),   value  = c(10, 8, 6, 4, 7) ) standardize_country(messy, nation, warn = FALSE) #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>   nation        value iso3c iso2c continent region                #>   <chr>         <dbl> <chr> <chr> <chr>     <chr>                 #> 1 U.S.             10 USA   US    Americas  North America         #> 2 S. Korea          8 KOR   KR    Asia      East Asia & Pacific   #> 3 Czechia           6 CZE   CZ    Europe    Europe & Central Asia #> 4 Kosovo            4 XKX   XK    Europe    Europe & Central Asia #> 5 Cote d'Ivoire     7 CIV   CI    Africa    Sub-Saharan Africa left  <- data.frame(country = c("Czechia", "South Korea"), gdp = c(1, 2)) right <- data.frame(nation  = c("Czech Republic", "Korea, Rep."), pop = c(10, 51)) country_join(left, right, country, nation) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   country       gdp iso3c nation           pop #>   <chr>       <dbl> <chr> <chr>          <dbl> #> 1 Czechia         1 CZE   Czech Republic    10 #> 2 South Korea     2 KOR   Korea, Rep.       51

what: Diagnostics: never lose a country silently
title: countryatlas: Joining World Data to Maps on the ISO Spine
text: check_country_match() pre-flight report; wdj_overrides() curated match table replaces old drop-list; audit_coverage() reports missingness half-empty map published. entities previous version dropped — Kosovo, Micronesia, Virgin Islands dozen others — now matched:
code: check_country_match(c("USA", "Cote d'Ivoire", "Yugoslavia", "Wakanda")) #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   input         iso3c matched suggestion #>   <chr>         <chr> <lgl>   <chr>      #> 1 USA           USA   TRUE    NA         #> 2 Cote d'Ivoire CIV   TRUE    NA         #> 3 Yugoslavia    NA    FALSE   Yugoslavia #> 4 Wakanda       NA    FALSE   Canada audit_coverage(snapshot)$na_rates #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   indicator           n n_missing na_rate #>   <chr>           <int>     <int>   <dbl> #> 1 gdp_per_capita    215         9  0.0419 #> 2 population        215         0  0      #> 3 life_expectancy   215         0  0      #> 4 co2_per_capita    215        12  0.0558 dropped <- c("Kosovo", "Micronesia", "Virgin Islands", "Canary Islands",              "Saint Martin") standardize_country(data.frame(region = dropped), region, warn = FALSE) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   iso3c iso2c continent region                    #>   <chr> <chr> <chr>     <chr>                     #> 1 XKX   XK    Europe    Europe & Central Asia     #> 2 FSM   FM    Oceania   East Asia & Pacific       #> 3 VIR   VI    Americas  Latin America & Caribbean #> 4 ESP   ES    Europe    Europe & Central Asia     #> 5 MAF   MF    Americas  Latin America & Caribbean

what: A map-ready tibble in one call
title: Getting started
text: live connection, world_data(year) returns everything need: keep vignette offline, use bundled snapshot attach geometry :
code: data_2020 <- world_data(2020) data_2020 <- attach_geometry(world_snapshot$countries, geometry = "polygon")

title: Getting started
text: geom_polygon() boilerplate — world_map() :  Income ordered factor, categorical fill reads naturally:
code: world_map(data_2020, gdp_per_capita, style = "quantile",           title = "GDP per capita") world_map(data_2020, income, style = "categorical")

title: Getting started
text: limited GDP. Pass World Bank indicator code (named, clean columns), browse bundled catalogue:
code: head(common_indicators) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   name                   code           description                        #>   <chr>                  <chr>          <chr>                              #> 1 population             SP.POP.TOTL    Population, total                  #> 2 gdp                    NY.GDP.MKTP.CD GDP (current US$)                  #> 3 gdp_constant           NY.GDP.MKTP.KD GDP (constant 2015 US$)            #> 4 gdp_per_capita         NY.GDP.PCAP.KD GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$) #> 5 gdp_per_capita_current NY.GDP.PCAP.CD GDP per capita (current US$)       #> 6 gni_per_capita         NY.GNP.PCAP.CD GNI per capita (current US$) country_data(2020, c(life_exp = "SP.DYN.LE00.IN", pop = "SP.POP.TOTL"))

title: Getting started
text: Joining data — get frame keyed messy names onto map. Modern maps sf & projections — equal-area, projected maps. Beyond choropleth — bubbles, cartograms, tiles, flows .

title: Joining your own data
text: standardize_country() attaches ISO codes classifications, reconciling spellings automatically:
code: my_data <- data.frame(   nation = c("U.S.", "S. Korea", "Czechia", "Kosovo", "Cote d'Ivoire", "UK"),   score  = c(10, 8, 6, 4, 7, 9) ) standardize_country(my_data, nation, warn = FALSE) #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>   nation        score iso3c iso2c continent region                #>   <chr>         <dbl> <chr> <chr> <chr>     <chr>                 #> 1 U.S.             10 USA   US    Americas  North America         #> 2 S. Korea          8 KOR   KR    Asia      East Asia & Pacific   #> 3 Czechia           6 CZE   CZ    Europe    Europe & Central Asia #> 4 Kosovo            4 XKX   XK    Europe    Europe & Central Asia #> 5 Cote d'Ivoire     7 CIV   CI    Africa    Sub-Saharan Africa    #> 6 UK                9 GBR   GB    Europe    Europe & Central Asia

title: Joining your own data
text: join_world() auto-detects country column, standardises attaches geometry:
code: my_data |>   join_world(nation, warn = FALSE) |>   world_map(score, title = "My data on the ISO spine")

title: Joining your own data
text: country_join() joins two frames key country names, reconciling sides iso3c first:
code: a <- data.frame(country = c("Czechia", "South Korea", "Russia"), gdp = 1:3) b <- data.frame(nation  = c("Czech Republic", "Korea, Rep.", "Russian Federation"),                 pop = c(10, 51, 144)) country_join(a, b, country, nation) #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>   country       gdp iso3c nation               pop #>   <chr>       <int> <chr> <chr>              <dbl> #> 1 Czechia         1 CZE   Czech Republic        10 #> 2 South Korea     2 KOR   Korea, Rep.           51 #> 3 Russia          3 RUS   Russian Federation   144

title: Joining your own data
text: Always inspect failed match: something legitimately matched, extend override table:
code: check_country_match(my_data$nation) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   input         iso3c matched suggestion #>   <chr>         <chr> <lgl>   <chr>      #> 1 U.S.          USA   TRUE    NA         #> 2 S. Korea      KOR   TRUE    NA         #> 3 Czechia       CZE   TRUE    NA         #> 4 Kosovo        XKX   TRUE    NA         #> 5 Cote d'Ivoire CIV   TRUE    NA         #> 6 UK            GBR   TRUE    NA wdj_overrides(c(Somaliland = "SOM"))[c("Kosovo", "Somaliland")] #>     Kosovo Somaliland  #>      "XKX"      "SOM"

title: Joining your own data
text: key already ISO-2 World Bank code, tell standardize_country() via origin:
code: df <- data.frame(code = c("US", "KR", "BR")) standardize_country(df, code, origin = "iso2c", warn = FALSE) #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>   code  iso3c iso2c continent region                    #>   <chr> <chr> <chr> <chr>     <chr>                     #> 1 US    USA   US    Americas  North America             #> 2 KR    KOR   KR    Asia      East Asia & Pacific       #> 3 BR    BRA   BR    Americas  Latin America & Caribbean

title: Modern maps with sf & projections
text: world_map() auto-detects sf backend applies projection ggplot2::coord_sf(). Available projections "equal_earth" (default — equal-area good-looking), "robinson", "mollweide", "natural_earth", "plate_carree", "mercator", "winkel_tripel", "eckert4", "gall_peters", "orthographic", "azimuthal_equal_area", "north_polar" "south_polar".
code: world_data(2020, c(gdp = "NY.GDP.PCAP.KD"), geometry = "sf") |>   world_map(gdp, style = "quantile", projection = "equal_earth",             title = "GDP per capita (Equal Earth projection)")

title: Modern maps with sf & projections
text: globe_map() draws orthographic globe centred lon/lat. default "sf" backend gives cleanest limb; "polygon" backend needs maps + mapproj (sf):  spin_globe() turns rotating animation — one globe_map() frame per central longitude, assembled looping GIF gifski (magick):
code: globe_map(world_snapshot$countries, continent, backend = "polygon",           style = "categorical", lon = 10, lat = 20) # With the sf backend (smoother limb, real great circles): world_data(2020, geometry = "sf") |>   globe_map(gdp_per_capita, lon = 10, lat = 30) spin_globe(world_snapshot$countries, continent, backend = "polygon",            style = "categorical", n_frames = 60)

title: Modern maps with sf & projections
text: world_geometry() returns projected, region-subset, antimeridian-safe geometry without data — country polygons, label-ready centroids, coastlines, graticule ocean rectangle:
code: africa <- world_geometry("countries", geometry = "sf", region = "Africa",                          projection = "equal_earth") ggplot(africa) +   geom_sf(fill = "grey85", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.1) +   theme_world_map()

title: Modern maps with sf & projections
text: Pacific-centred world one argument away; sf pipeline runs sf::st_break_antimeridian() projecting, nothing streaks across frame:
code: world_geometry("countries", geometry = "sf", recenter = 150)

title: Modern maps with sf & projections
text: region accepts continent, group name ("EU", "OECD", …), vector iso3c codes, bounding box c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax), picks sensible projection .

title: Modern maps with sf & projections
text: High-resolution geometry can thinned fast plotting simplify_geometry() (uses rmapshaper available).
code: world_geometry(geometry = "sf", scale = "large") |>   simplify_geometry(keep = 0.1)

title: countryatlas • Join world data to maps on the ISO spine
text: Country names never line across data sources. "US", "U.S.", "United States", "United States America" "America" country, naïve left_join() treats five. countryatlas kills pain making ISO codes universal join key handing single, ready--map tibble already stitches together three otherwise disjoint worlds: ggplot2::map_data("world") (Natural Earth sf) — countries , WDI — true (World Bank indicators), countrycode — Rosetta stone (ISO codes, continents, regions) makes join possible. happy path one call: world_data(2020). Everything else opt-.

title: countryatlas • Join world data to maps on the ISO spine
text: Render maps database ggsql: as_ggsql_source(), world_query(), interactive_map(engine = "ggsql"). map types: orthographic globe (globe_map()), small multiples (facet_map()), 8 projections (Winkel tripel, orthographic, Gall–Peters, …). Point data onto spine: locate_country() (point--polygon). Cleaner joins: repair_country_names() auto-fixes typos; country_join_all() reduce-joins many tables . analysis: growth_rate(), index_to(), share_of_world(). country groups: Mercosur, GCC, Nordic, Visegrád. Correctness fixes change map output (quantile binning, centroids, label placement, projections, override-lookups) — full changelog.

title: countryatlas • Join world data to maps on the ISO spine

what: Render in the database with ggsql
title: countryatlas • Join world data to maps on the ISO spine
text: ggsql draws plots database (DuckDB) returns Vega-Lite widget — ggplot2 sf runtime needed. countryatlas part ggsql’s static world can’t (ISO reconciliation, overrides, WDI join); ggsql part countryatlas doesn’t (push-+ web-ready output). world_query() emits spatial query (dependencies): …as_ggsql_source() / interactive_map(engine = "ggsql") register curated table render database. See countryatlas ggsql vignette.
code: world_query(gdp_per_capita, palette = "magma", transform = "log10",             title = "GDP per capita") #> VISUALISE gdp_per_capita AS fill #> FROM countryatlas_world #> DRAW spatial #> PROJECT TO equal_earth #> SCALE fill TO magma VIA log10 #> LABEL title => 'GDP per capita'

what: More ways in, more to compute
title: countryatlas • Join world data to maps on the ISO spine
text: Get point data onto spine, repair messy names, reduce-join many tables, run panel analysis — keyed iso3c: repair_country_names() auto-fixes typos closest known country, locate_country(lon, lat) tags coordinates country contains , growth_rate() / index_to() add panel metrics.
code: # each country's share of a world total (within year, for a panel) share_of_world(data.frame(iso3c = c("USA", "CHN", "IND"), co2 = c(5, 15, 3)), co2) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   iso3c   co2 co2_share #>   <chr> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 USA       5     0.217 #> 2 CHN      15     0.652 #> 3 IND       3     0.130  # reduce-join several messy tables on the ISO spine at once t1 <- data.frame(country = c("Czechia", "South Korea"), gdp = c(1, 2)) t2 <- data.frame(country = c("Czech Republic", "Korea, Rep."), pop = c(10, 51)) t3 <- data.frame(country = c("Czechia", "Korea"), area = c(79, 100)) country_join_all(list(t1, t2, t3), by = "country") #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   country.x     gdp iso3c country.y        pop country  area #>   <chr>       <dbl> <chr> <chr>          <dbl> <chr>   <dbl> #> 1 Czechia         1 CZE   Czech Republic    10 Czechia    79 #> 2 South Korea     2 KOR   Korea, Rep.       51 Korea     100

Implemented 2.0.0 (see NEWS.md): ggsql bridge (as_ggsql_source(), world_query(), interactive_map(engine = "ggsql")); eight bug fixes §3; projection expansion + globe_map() + spin_globe() + facet_map(); locate_country(), repair_country_names(), country_join_all(), growth_rate(), index_to(), share_of_world(), country_overrides(); four new country groups (Mercosur, GCC, Nordic, Visegrád). Non-plotting tests pass; viridis-based plotting tests run CI (viridisLite illegal-instruction crash dev node prevents running locally). Deferred later cycle (need live-API testing data curation, shipped blind): external data-source adapters — §2.1 (OWID / Eurostat / V-Dem); historical / dissolved-entity crosswalk — §2.2; subnational admin1 geometry — §2.3; disputed-territory de-facto/de-jure policy — §2.6; world_snapshot year refresh (needs World Bank API build time). Still open 2.0.0 — additional features worth building release ships collected new §2.7 ; rest document original plan, kept reference.

what: What ggsql is (so the integration is grounded)
title: countryatlas — next release planning (target: 2.0.0)
text: ggsql CRAN package posit-dev: “grammar graphics SQL.” lets describe plot inside SQL query (clauses VISUALISE / / DRAW / SCALE / PROJECT / FACET / LABEL / PLACE / MAPPING, SETTING), executes whole pipeline optimised SQL backend (DuckDB / SQLite today), returning Vega-Lite widget. what: 2.7 Additional features to add for 2.0.0 (new brainstorm)
title: countryatlas — next release planning (target: 2.0.0)
text: Beyond §2.1–2.6, following fresh candidates worth building 2.0.0 release. hang existing ISO spine + sf/WDI plumbing, self-contained. Tagged ★ high value / ◐ medium / ◦ nice--. sources & richer joins - ◐ Currency / real-terms helpers — deflate() (constant vs current prices via GDP-deflator/CPI series) to_ppp() / to_usd(). Country economic data almost always wanted real PPP terms; spine kills whole class silent unit mistakes. - ◐ Population-weighted aggregation — weight = / weighted = TRUE path aggregate_regions() regional means/medians weight population GDP instead country count. - ◦ Multilingual country names — convert_country(= "name_fr"/"name_es"/…) via countrycode’s language tables, localized labels joining non-English source data. Geometry & spatial structure - ★ Country adjacency / borders graph — country_borders() returning tidy neighbour list (/igraph), neighbors("FRA") distance_between(, b). Unlocks contiguity analysis “two neighbours share colour” map niceties. - ◐ Spatial autocorrelation — Moran’s / LISA world spine using adjacency (gated spdep Suggests); common ask choropleth users. - ◐ Multiple Natural Earth scales — expose scale = c("110m","50m","10m") consistently across world_geometry() maps, persisted cache_geometry(). - ◦ Inset maps — inset = TRUE helper breaking small/dense regions (Europe, Caribbean, Pacific SIDS) ’re actually legible. Visualization - ★ dorling_map() — promote Dorling cartogram first-class verb; add contiguous / non-contiguous cartograms sane defaults (extends §2.4). - ★ bivariate_legend() — finish bivariate_map() story standalone legend, palettes, binning controls. - ◐ spike_map() statebins-style standalone tile map beyond US-oriented tile_map(). - ◐ Animated transitions — animate_world(transition = "tween") years (gganimate), spin_globe() → MP4 output (just GIF). - ◦ Interactive WebGL globe — rgl/three.js globe complementing static globe_map() / spin_globe(). Analysis helpers - ★ correlate_indicators() — quick cross-indicator correlation/scatter spine (listed §2.5 yet shipped). - ◐ Convergence diagnostics — beta_convergence() / sigma_convergence(), pairing naturally shipped growth_rate() / index_to(). - ◐ Inequality measures — gini(), theil(), /within decomposition across countries (population-weighted). - ◐ Panel utilities — build_panel(), lag_by_country(), diff_by_country(), interpolate_missing() (linear/LOCF) panels join cleanly across patchy source coverage. ggsql, Shiny & reporting - ◐ Broaden ggsql render engine — extend world_query() / interactive_map(engine = "ggsql") spatial choropleths bubble/binned layers SCALE/FACET verbs ggsql spatial API stabilises. - ◐ Shiny module — worldMapInput() / worldMapServer() reconciled choropleth drops app two lines (ggplot / leaflet / ggsql engine). - ◦ gt / report helpers — country_factsheet() gt-formatted world_table() one-country top-N summaries.

what: 3.1 ★★★ world_map() quantile/jenks breaks are vertex-weighted, not country-weighted
title: countryatlas — next release planning (target: 2.0.0)
text: R/visualization.R:92-100 (compute_breaks() :33-45). Confirmed (R 4.4.1). polygon backend, data one row per boundary vertex, vals <- data[[fill_name]] repeats country’s value per vertex (tens thousands times). compute_breaks(vals, "quantile"/"jenks", n_bins) therefore computes quantiles vertices, weighting country geometric complexity. jagged country (Canada, Norway, Indonesia, UK islands) dominates breakpoints; “quantile” map longer roughly equal countries per colour — central promise quantile choropleth. (sf backend one row per country, ’s unaffected; binned range-based also unaffected. interactive_map()/animate_world() inherit bug via world_map().) Evidence: map_data("world") (vertex counts range 6 … 11,573 — Canada alone 11,573 vertices), 5-bin quantile choropleth synthetic skewed indicator put 62 / 60 / 28 / 69 / 32 countries five colour bins; country-weighted breaks put 51 / 50 / 50 / 50 / 50. “equal countries per colour” guarantee broken. Fix: compute breaks one value per country, cut full vector:
code: key  <- if ("iso3c" %in% names(data)) "iso3c" else "group" uvals <- dplyr::distinct(data, .data[[key]], .keep_all = TRUE)[[fill_name]] br <- compute_breaks(uvals, style, n_bins) data[[".wdj_bin"]] <- cut(vals, br, include.lowest = TRUE, dig.lab = 4)

what: 3.2 ★★★ bubble_map(backend = "sf") plots bubbles in projected metres on a degrees map
title: countryatlas — next release planning (target: 2.0.0)
text: R/visualization.R:187-211. High confidence (static — sf installed , code path unambiguous). backend = "sf", centroids come world_geometry("centroids", geometry = "sf", projection = ...) → coordinates projected metres (e.g. ±1.7e7). base layer always geom_polygon(world_geometry("countries", geometry = "polygon"), aes(long, lat)) + coord_quickmap() — .e. degrees (±180). bubbles render far outside map. backend = "sf" projection arguments effectively broken (polygon path “works” ignores projection entirely stays degrees). Fix: draw base map space centroids — either use sf base map coord_sf() backend = "sf", compute centroids lon/lat let coord_sf(crs = ...) projection.

what: 3.3 ★★ Duplicate polygon centroids fan out bubble_map() and flow_map()
title: countryatlas — next release planning (target: 2.0.0)
text: R/geometry.R:219-227 (polygon_centroids()), consumed R/visualization.R:193 :361-372. Confirmed (R 4.4.1): world_geometry("centroids", geometry = "polygon") returns >1 row 10 iso3c codes — BES PRT 3 (Bonaire/Saba/Sint Eustatius; Portugal/Azores/Madeira), ATG/ESP/IND/KNA/SGS/SHN/TTO/VCT 2. 1-row table c("PRT","ESP") joined centroids becomes 3 2 rows. polygon_centroids() group_by(iso3c, region) region map_data country-name column. wdj_overrides() deliberately maps several names one ISO code (Azores+Madeira→PRT, Canary Islands→ESP, Bonaire+Saba+Sint Eustatius→BES, …), result multiple centroid rows per iso3c. Downstream left_join(data, cent, = "iso3c") fans : bubble_map() draws several full-size bubbles countries (encoding whole national total), flow_map() draws duplicate arcs. Fix: return one centroid per iso3c (e.g. largest-ring centroid, data-raw/build_datasets.R:129-143 already computes — just reuse country_meta’s centroid_lon/lat), /distinct(iso3c) join.

what: 3.4 ★★ plate_carree builds an incoherent PROJ string
title: countryatlas — next release planning (target: 2.0.0)
text: R/geometry.R:11-19. Confirmed (R 4.4.1): wdj_crs("plate_carree") returns +proj=longlat +lon_0=0 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs — geographic CRS (degrees) tagged metres. st_transform() just un-projects, +lon_0= recentering doesn’t behave like real plate carrée. Plate carrée equirectangular = +proj=eqc. Fix: plate_carree = "+proj=eqc +lat_ts=0" (let shared +units=m apply correctly). Roll projection expansion §2.3.

what: 3.5 ★★ geom_country_labels() centroid breaks for antimeridian/multi-part countries
title: countryatlas — next release planning (target: 2.0.0)
text: R/visualization.R:551-564. Confirmed (R 4.4.1) — worse expected. Labels placed mean(range(long)), mean(range(lat)) country’s vertices, country crossing antimeridian lands label wrong ocean. Measured bounding-box centroids: USA → lon 0.8 (Alaska’s Aleutians cross 180°, dragging Atlantic Africa), Fiji → lon 0.2, New Zealand → lon 0.8. (data-raw already uses better largest-ring centroid; runtime path doesn’t.) root cause crude centroid 3.3. Fix: reuse country_meta centroids largest-polygon approach; share one centroid implementation across polygon_centroids(), labels layer, bubble_map()/flow_map().

what: 3.7 ◦ convert_country() overrides only apply when to == "iso3c"
title: countryatlas — next release planning (target: 2.0.0)
text: R/reference.R:56-66 (gate :61). Confirmed (R 4.4.1). override gate sets custom_match == "iso3c", override-entities (names countrycode doesn’t recognise natively) return NA every derived destination. Measured: convert_country("Canary Islands", = "iso3c") → ESP, convert_country("Canary Islands", = "continent") → NA ("Azores" → PRT / NA). standardize_country() gets right (Europe) routes iso3c first. (Note: many names recognised natively — convert_country("Kosovo", = "flag") works — gate bites override-names, exactly ’s easy miss.) Fix: resolve iso3c (overrides) first, convert iso3c → dest.

title: countryatlas 2.0.0
text: major release wires countryatlas database-rendering world via ‘ggsql’, widens map vocabulary, fixes several correctness issues found auditing 1.0.0. version bumped 2.0.0 bug fixes change output world_map() (quantile binning), bubble_map() / flow_map() (de-duplicated symbols), geom_country_labels() (label placement) convert_country() (override-entities) — code depended old behaviour may see different maps values.

title: countryatlas 2.0.0
text: as_ggsql_source() exports curated, ISO-reconciled, WDI-joined table (sf geometry WKB-encoded) ggsql source — DuckDB connection, Parquet file, nanoarrow stream. countryatlas reconciliation ggsql’s static bundled world can’t; ggsql database push-Vega-Lite output countryatlas doesn’t. world_query() emits ggsql spatial query (VISUALISE … DRAW spatial PROJECT … SCALE … LABEL …) — dependency-free string builder. interactive_map(engine = "ggsql") registers data renders map DuckDB, returning Vega-Lite widget. ggsql, duckdb, DBI nanoarrow optional Suggests. See new countryatlas ggsql vignette.

title: countryatlas 2.0.0
text: globe_map() — orthographic globe choropleth, backend = "sf" (smoothest limb) backend = "polygon" (needs maps + mapproj). spin_globe() — rotating-globe animated GIF (one globe_map() frame per central longitude, assembled gifski magick). facet_map() — small-multiple choropleths (static counterpart animate_world()). wdj_crs() gains eight projections (mercator, winkel_tripel, eckert4, gall_peters, orthographic, azimuthal_equal_area, north_polar, south_polar); world_map() / world_geometry() accept . locate_country() — point--polygon lookup tagging lon/lat iso3c. repair_country_names() — “act ” companion check_country_match(): auto-applies confident string-distance fixes. country_join_all() — reduce-join many messy country tables ISO spine. growth_rate(), index_to(), share_of_world() — panel analysis helpers. country_overrides() — preferred name wdj_overrides() (kept alias) rename countryatlas. country_groups_tbl gains Mercosur, GCC, Nordic Visegrad.

title: countryatlas 2.0.0
text: world_map(style = "quantile"/"jenks") computed breaks polygon vertices, country’s geometric complexity biased quantiles bins held unequal numbers countries. Breaks now computed one value per country. bubble_map(backend = "sf") placed bubbles projected metres degrees base map (map). base map bubbles now share one projected CRS via coord_sf(). Polygon centroids returned one row ten iso3c codes (overrides map several names — Azores/Madeira → PRT — one code), fanning joins bubble_map() / flow_map(). Centroids now one antimeridian-safe row per country (largest piece). geom_country_labels() placed labels bounding-box midpoint country’s pieces, US / Fiji / NZ labels drifted wrong ocean. Labels now sit country’s largest piece. projection = "plate_carree" built incoherent PROJ string (+proj=longlat … +units=m); now true equirectangular (+proj=eqc). convert_country() applied wdj_overrides() = "iso3c", override-entities (e.g. “Canary Islands”) returned NA derived destinations. now routes corrected iso3c first.

title: countryatlas 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-24 single, comprehensive release takes package one-function proof concept complete toolkit joining world data maps. spirit unchanged — ISO codes universal join key, one call map-ready table — pushed full potential.

title: countryatlas 1.0.0
text: world_data() generalised backward-compatible: world_data(2020) still returns classic polygon-backed, GDP-per-capita tibble. visible change column name gdp_per_capita_2015 → gdp_per_capita. one-cycle deprecation shim keeps gdp_per_capita_2015 available alias (toggle options(countryatlas.gdp_compat = FALSE)). 16 regions previous version silently dropped (Kosovo, Micronesia, Virgin Islands, Saint Martin, Bonaire/Saba/Sint Eustatius, Canary Islands, Madeira/Azores, …) now matched via [wdj_overrides()] instead deleted, appear maps. Diffs map output show increased coverage.

title: countryatlas 1.0.0
